CASTLE (CAusal STructure LEarning Regularization)

Source: R/causalDeepNet.R
castle.Rd

Torch implementation of CASTLE translated from the Python tutorial module: learn weighted adjacency \(A\), optimize prediction + sparsity + acyclicity (NOTEARS-style) + neighborhood reconstruction regularization.

Usage

castle(
  X,
  y,
  hidden_dim = 64L,
  num_layers = 3L,
  lambda_reg = 1,
  beta_sparsity = 0.1,
  acyc_weight = 1,
  recon_weight = 0.5,
  neighbor_temp = 10,
  y_index = NULL,
  epochs = 200L,
  batch_size = 128L,
  learning_rate = 0.001,
  weight_decay = 1e-05,
  verbose = TRUE,
  device = NULL,
  threshold = 0.05,
  ...
)

Arguments

X

Numeric matrix or data.frame of predictors.

y

Numeric outcome.

hidden_dim

Hidden layer width (default 64).

num_layers

Number of predictor layers (default 3).

lambda_reg

Weight on prediction loss (default 1).

beta_sparsity

L1 weight on adjacency (default 0.1).

acyc_weight

Acyclicity penalty weight (default 1).

recon_weight

Neighborhood reconstruction weight (default 0.5).

neighbor_temp

Temperature for neighborhood soft mask (default 10).

y_index

1-based index of outcome column in Z=[X,y]. Default last column.

epochs

Number of training epochs (default 200).

batch_size

Batch size (default 128).

learning_rate

Adam learning rate (default 1e-3).

weight_decay

Adam weight decay (default 1e-5).

verbose

If TRUE, print progress every 20 epochs.

device

Device string: "cuda", "cpu", or NULL.

threshold

Threshold used by summary/plot for edge filtering (default 0.05).

...

Ignored.

Value

Object of class castle.

See also

RCausalML-package

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# castle(...)
} # }
